name: Marco Rubio
subject: Wait for what?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marco Rubio for President
body:
| | | | | | | | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/44644197:6tdzKILNL:m:1:1349778030:B19649B22C373C29B2D46EF58F3BA08B:r)
---
| | | | Peter, Last night, in the latest Republican presidential debate, one of the moderators actually asked me if I should "slow down." That's exactly what the establishment has been telling me for years. That I should "wait my turn." Wait for what? This country is running out of time:
* Millions of people are living paycheck to paycheck at the same time they're working as hard as they ever have because everything costs more. They have not had a real raise in decades.
* Small businesses are struggling: more businesses are closing than opening.
* We live in a world that's out of control, with a president who's weakening our military and doubling down on a failed foreign policy while our adversaries continue to grow stronger.

The time to act is now, the time to turn the page is now. If we don't act now, we'll be the first generation that leaves our children worse off than ourselves. **[> >Click here to donate $10 if you agree that the time to act is now!<<](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/44644198:6tdzKILNL:m:1:1349778030:B19649B22C373C29B2D46EF58F3BA08B:r)** With your help and support, I'll be the leader that turns our country around and ensures the 21st Century is an American Century. **But you saw last night just how difficult our task will be with the mainstream media acting as Hillary Clinton's Super PAC.** If you want to ensure that our campaign can take the truth straight to the voters -- without relying on coverage from the biased media we've come to expect (and saw last night) -- **[I'm asking you to chip in $10 to our campaign as a personal favor to me.](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/44644198:6tdzKILNL:m:1:1349778030:B19649B22C373C29B2D46EF58F3BA08B:r)** Sincerely, Marco Rubio P.S. I couldn't believe it when one of the moderators misled about my tax plan -- despite having to correct a story earlier this month where he made the exact same claim! [**Your financial support enables us to level the playing field.**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/44644198:6tdzKILNL:m:1:1349778030:B19649B22C373C29B2D46EF58F3BA08B:r)

[**If you have 2 minutes, I really need your opinion on last night's debate.**](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/31320298:WXV4hgmdN:m:1:876315805:C04904E959DB712B1AFA44E1B426B53D:r)

name: Rand Paul
subject: filibustering the establishment's budget deal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Dear Supporter:

Last night, I put the Washington Machine on notice.

Under no circumstances will they get away with RAMMING yet another smoky backroom deal to send our $18.4 TRILLION national debt skyrocketing and spending through the roof without a fight.

Instead, with your help, I'm going to filibuster and do everything in my power to STOP this outrageous scheme.

But with this fight ready to reach a fever pitch in just a matter of days, I must have your IMMEDIATE support.

**[Will you please agree to your most generous contribution of $20.16 to help me reach and mobilize even more Americans](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/31321252:W85gJgmLN:m:1:879035888:312601B4FC5BDC147D8930F9CBD8DC0A:r)**?

During last night's Republican debate, the American people got a firsthand glimpse of how our national media stands ready to skewer anyone who challenges the Big Government bipartisan consensus in Washington, D.C.

The moderators had nothing but kind words to say about the outrageous "deal" President Obama and outgoing House Speaker John Boehner hatched in secret to raise the country's debt limit through 2017 and send government spending through the roof.

But their behavior throughout the debate -- which stood out as outrageous even for our national media -- has created a firestorm of well-deserved anger.

After all, the American people understand we can't keep spending like there's no tomorrow.

Today, our nation is $18.4 TRILLION in debt!

How high will the big spenders in Congress send it soaring if we allow this scheme to pass?

Your guess is as good as mine.

Since this debt deal effectively raises the nation's debt limit indefinitely through 2017, it's like handing BOTH parties a blank check to do their worst until after the 2016 elections!

With control of both houses of Congress, Republicans should be using the debt limit fight to achieve real cuts in spending.

Instead, my colleagues seem all too willing to wave the white flag.

As long as they can keep doling out dollars to their cronies, BOTH parties are willing to spend like there's no tomorrow.

Well, at some point, there won't be a tomorrow if we allow this madness to continue.

That's why I'm going to do everything in my power to stop this outrageous debt deal.

**[Will you please agree to Stand With Rand during this critical filibuster, and help me stop this madness](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/31321252:W85gJgmLN:m:1:879035888:312601B4FC5BDC147D8930F9CBD8DC0A:r)**?

Will you agree to a donation of $20.16 or perhaps even more?

Time is running out. I'm counting on you.

Under no circumstances will they get away with RAMMING yet another smoky backroom deal to send our $18.4 TRILLION national debt skyrocketing and spending through the roof without a fight. But with this fight ready to reach a fever pitch in just a matter of days, I must have your IMMEDIATE support. ? During last night's Republican debate, the American people got a firsthand glimpse of how our national media stands ready to skewer anyone who challenges the Big Government bipartisan consensus in Washington, D.C. The moderators had nothing but kind words to say about the outrageous "deal" President Obama and outgoing House Speaker John Boehner hatched in secret to raise the country's debt limit through 2017 and send government spending through the roof. But their behavior throughout the debate -- which stood out as outrageous even for our national media -- has created a firestorm of well-deserved anger. After all, the American people understand we can't keep spending like there's no tomorrow. Today, our nation is $18.4 TRILLION in debt! How high will the big spenders in Congress send it soaring if we allow this scheme to pass? Your guess is as good as mine. Since this debt deal effectively raises the nation's debt limit indefinitely through 2017, it's like handing BOTH parties a blank check to do their worst until after the 2016 elections! With control of both houses of Congress, Republicans should be using the debt limit fight to achieve real cuts in spending. Instead, my colleagues seem all too willing to wave the white flag. It’s not easy standing out on a debate stage filled with 10 candidates, many of whom rely on bombast and empty rhetoric to get attention, but John Kasich stood strong for us, vowing fix big problems with real solutions. [Watch the highlights:](http://click.icptrack.com/icp/relay.php?r=35444401&msgid=104563&act=N28F&c=1559159&destination=https%3A%2F%2Fnewdayforamerica.com%2Fdebatewin%2F) [****](http://click.icptrack.com/icp/relay.php?r=35444401&msgid=104563&act=N28F&c=1559159&destination=https%3A%2F%2Fnewdayforamerica.com%2Fdebatewin%2F) John Kasich created buzz for all the right reasons. _The debate winner was Kasich, the Ohio Governor who grabbed the spotlight at the sometimes noisy and chaotic two-hour session. He was strong and unflustered._
_WINNERS: Gov. John Kasich: Right from the opening bell, he was an aggressor who assailed the math of the economic plans of frontrunners Mr. Carson and Mr. Trump. Thanks for all you're doing for our team. **Matt Carle**

body:
| [ ](http://www.ricksantorum.com/?e=84b355b85f9e9360b9dac4e2a50bbee5&utm_source=santorum&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=cheapskate&n=1)
---
Friend --
Dirty little secret...Rick is a cheapskate!

Unlike some campaigns that fly in fancy private jets and stay in five-star hotels, Rick Santorum’s campaign flies economy class and he stays at budget motels. He doesn’t waste his campaign money, he spends it on things that will actually help him win votes.

And that’s exactly why he won Iowa four years ago and it’s why he is well positioned to win it again! But each trip to Iowa still comes with a cost:
* It costs **$12.99 to buy a pizza for supporters** at a town hall meeting at one of Iowa’s famous Pizza Ranch restaurants.

* It costs **$35.85 to buy a tank of gas for our team** to travel to a local GOP meeting or community event.

* It costs **$52.95 for the Senator to stay at the Days Inn** when he visits Des Moines.

* And it will cost **$250 to buy campaign signs, print materials to hand out, and to train one of our supporters** to speak on behalf of the Senator at each of the 1,700 Iowa Caucus locations!

[Will you support our Iowa team today by sponsoring refreshments at a town hall meeting, a tank of gas for our staff, or even a Caucus site this February?](https://santorum.nationbuilder.com/cheapskate?e=84b355b85f9e9360b9dac4e2a50bbee5&utm_source=santorum&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=cheapskate&n=2)

With your support, we will add you to an exclusive list of supporters who will receive on-the-ground updates from a member of our Iowa team. Each week you will receive a behind-the-scenes look at our preparations for victory this February.

The race is heating up, the polls are beginning to move and we are working hard to put together the ground game Rick Santorum needs to defy the Beltway Experts and win the Iowa Caucus again... but he needs your help today!

Thank you for your support,

Walt Rogers
Iowa State Director
Santorum for President 2016

P.S. - You may not live in Iowa, but your support today will go a long way to helping Rick Santorum once again surprise the Establishment and win the Iowa Caucus.

With your support, we will add you to an exclusive list of supporters who will receive on-the-ground updates from a member of our Iowa team. Each week you will receive a behind-the-scenes look at our preparations for victory this February. Thank you for your support, Walt Rogers Iowa State Director Santorum for President 2016 P.S. - You may not live in Iowa, but your support today will go a long way to helping Rick Santorum once again surprise the Establishment and win the Iowa Caucus. name: Jeff Weaver, Bernie 2016
subject: You are our Super PACK
body:
#
Friend -
Almost four months from today on March 1, eleven Super Tuesday states will hold primaries and caucuses at once. Now, we're doing quite well in the "first four" states, but in order to win the Democratic nomination, we simply have to continue growing our political revolution.
Here's the straight truth: we will be outspent. We've been attacked by two separate super PACs already, and I'm sure there will be more coming as we rise in the polls. By the time we're done, there will be untold millions spent against our campaign.
**Bernie doesn 't have a super PAC.** That means we rely on individuals -- hundreds of thousands of them -- contributing small amounts of money at a time to fund our political revolution. **You are part of the Super PACK who will carry us to victory in the primary, general election, and take back our country from the billionaire class.**
[**Add your $3 monthly recurring contribution to become a member of Bernie 's Super PACK today. If you do, we'll send you this sticker in the mail:**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb24b3/43f17890/52880e49/621f2a23/3669424410/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/our-super-pack?refcode=em151029-full&recurring=yes")
[](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb24b3/43f17890/52880e49/621f2a23/3669424410/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/our-super-pack?refcode=em151029-full&recurring=yes")
Right now, we're just short of 100,000 people having signed up to make monthly recurring contributions through Super Tuesday, and it's important that we pass that mark. Doing so will allow us to be secure in the knowledge we'll have the resources needed to win.
People should not underestimate us.
Jeff Weaver
Campaign Manager
Bernie 2016

subject: Re: filibustering the establishment's budget deal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Supporter,

You know, I left my medical practice and ran
for office because I was concerned about an $18 trillion debt.

We borrow a million dollars a minute. Now, in
"conservative" Washington, they put forward a bill that will
explode the deficit.

It allows President Obama to borrow unlimited
amounts of money.

I will stand firm and spend every ounce of
energy I have to stop it.

But I need your immediate support.

Will you please agree to your most generous
contribution of $20.16 to help me reach and mobilize even more
Americans?

You and I cannot allow the mainstream media
and the Washington Machine to drown out our message of liberty
and limited government.

With control of both houses of Congress,
Republicans should be using the debt limit fight to achieve real
cuts in spending.

Instead, my colleagues seem all too willing
to wave the white flag.

As long as they can keep doling out dollars
to their cronies, BOTH parties are willing to spend like there's
no tomorrow.

Well, at some point, there won't be a
tomorrow if we allow this madness to continue.

That's why I'm going to do everything in my
power to stop this outrageous debt deal. But I cannot do this
without you.

It's critical you call your Senators to
oppose the debt ceiling deal! The first vote could come
overnight to cut off my filibuster. Let your Senators know you
want them to vote "NO!" by calling them at (202) 224-3121.

After you call, please chip in a generous
contribution of $20.16 right away.

Please don't wait, Supporter. I'm really
counting on your support.

For more information, please read my email
below.

In Liberty,

Rand Paul

---------- Begin Forwarded Message ----------

Subject: filibustering the establishment's
budget deal
From: Rand Paul
To:
Supporter

Dear Supporter:

Last night, I put the Washington Machine on
notice.

Under no circumstances will they get away
with RAMMING yet another smoky backroom deal to send our $18.4
TRILLION national debt skyrocketing and spending through the roof
without a fight.

Instead, with your help, I'm going to
filibuster and do everything in my power to STOP this outrageous
scheme.

But with this fight ready to reach a fever
pitch in just a matter of days, I must have your IMMEDIATE
support.

Will you please agree to your most generous
contribution of $20.16 to help me reach and mobilize even more
Americans?

During last night's Republican debate, the
American people got a firsthand glimpse of how our national media
stands ready to skewer anyone who challenges the Big Government
bipartisan consensus in Washington, D.C.

The moderators had nothing but kind words to
say about the outrageous "deal" President Obama and outgoing
House Speaker John Boehner hatched in secret to raise the
country's debt limit through 2017 and send government spending
through the roof.

But their behavior throughout the debate --
which stood out as outrageous even for our national media -- has
created a firestorm of well-deserved anger.

After all, the American people understand we
can't keep spending like there's no tomorrow.

Today, our nation is $18.4 TRILLION in debt!

How high will the big spenders in Congress
send it soaring if we allow this scheme to pass?

Your guess is as good as mine.

Since this debt deal effectively raises the
nation's debt limit indefinitely through 2017, it's like handing
BOTH parties a blank check to do their worst until after the 2016
elections!

With control of both houses of Congress,
Republicans should be using the debt limit fight to achieve real
cuts in spending.

Instead, my colleagues seem all too willing
to wave the white flag.

As long as they can keep doling out dollars
to their cronies, BOTH parties are willing to spend like there's
no tomorrow.

Well, at some point, there won't be a
tomorrow if we allow this madness to continue.

That's why I'm going to do everything in my
power to stop this outrageous debt deal.

Will you please agree to Stand With Rand
during this critical filibuster, and help me stop this madness?

Will you agree to a donation of $20.16 or
perhaps even more?

Time is running out. I'm counting on you.

Chip in $10
immediately >>

Chip in $25
immediately >>

Chip in $50
immediately >>

Chip in $100
immediately >>

Or donate another
amount >>

In Liberty,

Rand Paul

name: Team Pataki
subject: Governor Pataki's Greatest Hits
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pataki for President, Inc
body:
-
http://cts.vresp.com/c/?WWLLC/8135b2d0c0/ace65cf3c9/1a835d0c4e/sc=1029eb
Friend,

Governor Pataki is getting an overwhelmingly positive response for
his performance in last night's CNBC debate. We wanted to make sure
you saw these quotes and clips from the debate:

On tax reform:
"I cut taxes more than everybody on this stage, more than everybody
on the next stage combined, by more than the other 49 states, in New
York State. I don't just have a plan. I will enact tax cuts, get rid
of those loopholes, and make the system fairer for all Americans."

-
http://cts.vresp.com/c/?WWLLC/8135b2d0c0/ace65cf3c9/838d21ad04/video=3000439418

On working across party lines:
"I understand to change Washington, you have to understand government
as well. You can't just be an outsider, you can't just be someone who
throws stones at Washington. You have to be someone who can actually
bring people together across party lines. I can do that. I will do
that if I have the chance to lead this party."

On protecting America's military:
"Barack Obama is the first President in American history to hold our
military hostage. He knew that we needed funding for overseas
contingent operations, $30 million that would go to support our
troops. And he was prepared and would have vetoed it unless this deal
went through. I have two sons. They both served overseas, one in Iraq
and one in Afghanistan. I understand we have got to do far more to
help our military, help our veterans and help protect our security.
This is a bad deal. But to protect our military, I would have signed
it."

-
http://cts.vresp.com/c/?WWLLC/8135b2d0c0/ace65cf3c9/c134e5a1ca

On Hillary Clinton:
"Hillary Clinton put a server, an unsecure server, in her home as
Secretary of State. We have no doubt that that was hacked and that
state secrets are out there to the Iranians, the Russians, the
Chinese, and others. That, alone, should disqualify her from being
President of the United States."

On Obamacare:
"No question, Barack Obama inherited an economic disaster in 2009.
But what did he do? Instead of focusing on pro-growth policies and
the economy, he ran through Obamacare, the worst law of my lifetime,
that hurt small businesses, hurt companies, raised taxes, and almost
completely eliminated one industry because of its taxes."

On vaccines and climate change:
"One of the things that troubles me about the Republican Party is too
often we question science that everyone accepts. I mean, it's
ridiculous in the 21st century we are questioning whether or not
vaccines are the appropriate way to go. Of course they are. And it's
also not appropriate to think that human activity, putting CO2 into
the atmosphere, doesn't make the earth warmer, all things being
equal. It does. It's uncontroverted."

-
http://cts.vresp.com/c/?WWLLC/8135b2d0c0/ace65cf3c9/7e8398b333

On a new brand of conservatism:
"I'm a limited government conservative. And I mean by that that not
just when it comes to economic issues, leaving them to the state, but
social issues as well. And in that I differ from every single other
candidate seeking the Republican nomination. I take the Tenth
Amendment very, very seriously."

On embracing science and innovation:
"I'm a Republican who embraces science and understands we have to
work with the next generation of Millennials to have the innovation
and technology so that we can grow a 21st century economy."

On supporting America's blue-collar workers:
"What we have to do in America is honor blue-collar work again. We
have to honor the carpenter, the plumber, the electrician who can
actually build something. And instead of just saying that a college
degree delivers prestige, let's celebrate those who do things with
their hands and elevate their skills, using training in high school
and community colleges."

On Uber:
"The [app] I use the most is Uber. You know, I used to get driven.
When I was governor, I had a driver. I don't anymore. It's an example
of what Millennials are doing to change America for the better."

On New York sports teams:
"I'm a long-suffering Jets fan, so my answer is no, there's no reason
to take off the day after the Super Bowl...Let me just add this. The
Mets are going to win tonight."

Team Pataki

PS: Help us keep our momentum going forward after last night's debate
victory by contributing $5, $10, $50, $100, $1,000, or even $2,700
today! -

-
